posts.title: Manish Wahi
posts.author: Manish Wahi 2B9923
posts.topic: As 2026 begins, I’m grateful for a moment that truly stands out — being recognized as the Legend of the Quarter, the first award under Dubai Holding's new Rewards & Recognition programme. While I’m deeply honored, this recognition is truly a reflection of the incredible teams, stakeholders, and part
posts.ready_comment: Recognition programs like this one carry real weight when they're built around genuine team impact, not just individual performance metrics. Scaling complex operations across large, distributed organizations, whether that's managing digital transformation across 15 hospital facilities or aligning cross-functional teams under a shared vision, rarely succeeds without the kind of culture that makes people feel seen. The fact that Dubai Holding launched this as a structured program rather than an ad-hoc gesture tells me leadership there understands that retention and performance are deeply connected to acknowledgment. What criteria did they use to define "Legend" and how did they balance individual contribution against team outcomes?

posts.title: #rakbank #customerexperience #cxleadership ...
posts.author: Haitham Hashish 86351590
posts.topic: RAKBANK Proud moment with the team at our Annual Excellence Celebration ... Digital Transformation | Driving Efficiency and Growth at RAKBANK. 3h
posts.ready_comment: Celebrating the people behind the transformation is what separates sustainable change from short-term projects. Running a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals taught me that recognition culture directly influences execution speed, especially when teams are navigating complex, multi-stakeholder environments. The human side of technology leadership rarely gets enough spotlight, but it is often the deciding factor in whether transformation actually sticks. What has been the biggest cultural shift your team had to make to keep pace with the digital agenda at RAKBANK?
